semanticCore: Necessary desire, purpose, aim, marriage need, or target
quranicRelevance: Fulfilling marriage necessity/purpose (Watar - 'qadha zaydun minha wataran').

keyDerivations.meaning: Necessary desire, purpose, aim, vital marriage need, objective, or target

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَلَمَّا قَضَىٰ زَيْدٌ مِنْهَا وَطَرًا زَوَّجْنَاكَهَا (33:37): So when Zayd had fulfilled his desire/purpose regarding her (divorce completed), We married her to you (to break the taboo of marrying ex-wives of adopted sons).
discardedNoise: Aiming for home targets in archery.
summaryEn: The root represents fulfilling vital purposes and marriage desires (Watar), documenting the legal dissolution of Zayd's marriage to permit the Prophet's exemplary marriage.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الحاجة النفسية الملحّة والهدف المرغوب الضروري (وطر)، مخلداً إتمام زيد لحاجة زواجه بالطلاق لتشريع كسر تابوهات الأدعياء.
rawText: وَطَرٌ A want; an object of want or need: (Zj, S, A, Msb, K:) or one which one purposes to accomplish: (Lth:) or for which one is anxious, (A, K,) or desirous: (Msb:) pl. أَوْطَارٌ. (S, A, Msb, K.) It has no verb. (Kh, S, Msb.) You say, قَضَيْتُ وَطَرِى I accomplished, or obtained, my want, &c. (A, Msb, K.) And قَضَيْتُ مِنْ كَذَا وَطَرِى I accomplished my want of such a thing [so as to have no further need of it: see Kur, xxxiii. 37, in two places]. (Lth.)
